folio.label: 68r
folio.l: He putte it , in oure owen Iugement´
For had god comaunded , maydenheede
Than had he dampned , weddyng witú the dede
And certes if there were , no seede I sowe
Virginite than , wher of shuld it growe
Poule durst nat comaunden , at the leste
A thyngö of whicú , his maister ©af noon heeste
The dart is sette vp , for virginitee
Cacche who so may , who rennetú best lat se
But this word is nat take , of euäy wygút
But there as god wol ©eue it , of his mygút
I wot wel that the Appostel , was a mayde
But nathelees , thougú that he wroot or sayde
He wolde æ euäy wygút , were swicú as he
Al nys but counseille , to virginitee
And for to be a wyf , he ©af me leeue
Of indulgence , so is noon repreeue
To wedde me , if that my make deye
Witú oute excepcione , of bygamye

note.notetxt: 86excepcione, double check the manuscript. I am not convinced by the second c.ODR

folio.l: Al were it good , no womman for to touche
He ment in his bed , or in his couche
For peril is , bothe tow and fyr , to assemble
ñe knowe what this ensaumple , may resemble
This is al and sum , he held virginitee
More ¹fyt , than weddynge in freeletee
Freeltee clepe I , but ifif that he and she
Wold here lyues lede , al in chastitee
I graunte it wel , I haue noon envye
Thougú maydenhede , preferre bygamye
It liketú hem to be clene , in body and goost
Of myn estat , I wol nat maken boost
For wel ©e knowe , a lord in his houshold
Ne hatú nat euäy vessel , al of gold
Su~me ben of tree , and don here lord seruyse
God clepetú folk , to hym in sundry wyse
And euericú hatú of god , a propre ©ifte
Som this , som that , as æ hi~ liketú shifte
Virginite is , a greet ¹fecciouö
And continence eke , witú deuociouö
But Crist , that of ¹feccion , is welle
Ne bad nat euäy wygút , he shuld selle
Al æat he had , and ©eue it to the poore
And in swicú wyse , folwe hi~ and his foore
He spak to hem , that wold leue ¹fitly
And lordynges by ©oure leeue , that am not I
